title: Stakeholder Task, Workflow Fit, Authority, Risk, and Evidence · Behavior Case: Stakeholder Task
summary: A sponsor asks for an autonomous assistant but has not defined the user decision, accepted failure, permitted actions, affected people, oversight, or why a deterministic workflow is insufficient. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the stakeholder task increment in bounded agent product and risk charter. Learners progress through predic…
objectives: Define stakeholder, user population, decision, task distribution, observable value, failure, review cadence, and non-goals before choosing a model.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example agent engineering team handles case 3027 during a clean package, canary, and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate agent construction on stakeholder value, workflow fit, authority, measurable failure, risk controls, and escalation; model output and tool arguments remain untrusted until validated; then introduce a sensitive action, poisoned memory, leaking log, or computer-use request and deny or pause it. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Competency probe: Define stakeholder, user population, decision, task distribution, observable value, failure, review cadence, and non-goals before choosing a model. A defensible solution applies Stakeholder Task, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded agent product and risk charter.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-product-risk-contract-5
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-product-risk-contract-5.
# Case 9036: a privacy-minimized nonprofit workflow.
# Operating constraint: last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure.
# Changed case: introduce a sensitive action, poisoned memory, leaking log, or computer-use request and deny or pause it.
def worked_agentpy_product_risk_contract_5(authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 8, risk_controls: int = 6, unresolved_high_risks: int = 0, escalation_paths: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "authorityworked5",
        "interaction9036",
        "tool5dekrow",
        "stateworked59036",
        "evaluation9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "authority-missing"
    if acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "outcome-underdefined"
    if risk_controls < 4 or unresolved_high_risks > 0:
        return False, risk_controls, "risk-uncontrolled"
    if escalation_paths < 2:
        return False, escalation_paths, "escalation-missing"
    assert authorized and acceptance_cases >= 4 and unresolved_high_risks == 0
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-agent-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic fixed-fixture pure-Python agent decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected model, interaction, schema, tool, authority, state, approval, memory, evaluation, security, telemetry, sandbox, deployment, or recovery case, then state which provider, Gemini, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, identity, storage, browser, accessibility, privacy, load, cost, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Build Stakeholder Task evidence · Theory Stakeholder Task
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-stakeholder-task-1o36uc" that uses case 2364 to gate agent construction on stakeholder value, workflow fit, authority, measurable failure, risk controls, and escalation. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.
